rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT DAVID KALLMEYER , AN INDIVIDUAL, MICHAEL COMAROTO, GFDS ENGINEERS MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA SHEN-URQUIDEZ ET AL VS. LOUISA HU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Appraiser To Determine Fair Market Value Under Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT LOUISA HU NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT APPRAISER TO DETERMINE FAIR MARKET VALUE UNDER PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is GRANTED. The parties have not agreed on a fair market value or alternative method of valuation. Plaintiff has not adduced sufficient facts showing that Ms. Saling is biased. Ms. Saling shall be appointed as appraiser to determine the fair market value of the subject property. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CLAY KEARNY MERCHANT CORP. VS. LEONARD WALDON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2025 line 10. DEFENDANT LEONARD WALDON III (SUED AS LEONARD WALDON) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order. The Court notes that the amount which defendant asserts is his current lawful rent is not apparent from the face of the Complaint. However, based on the initial rent amount of $699 per month as of June 20, 2022 and the fact that the tenancy is subject to rent control, facts which are alleged on the face of the Complaint, the amount of unpaid rent allegedly due for one month as stated in the Three-Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit is plainly inconsistent. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
